title: Worker that chains follow-up jobs from a successful perform
prompt: Write `MyApp.Workers.OrderConfirmationWorker` (queue `:orders`, max_attempts 5). perform/1 takes `%{"order_id" => id}` and calls `MyApp.Orders.confirm(id)`. On `{:ok, order}`, it must enqueue two follow-up jobs atomically: `MyApp.Workers.InventoryReserveWorker.new(%{"order_id" => order.id})` and `MyApp.Workers.EmailConfirmationWorker.new(%{"order_id" => order.id})`. Use `Oban.insert_all/1` with a LIST of changesets (not insert_all with args, note this is the plural form for multiple jobs). Return `:ok` on success, `{:error, reason}` on failure. Write to `lib/my_app/workers/order_confirmation_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: Tests knowledge of `Oban.insert_all/1` for bulk insert of pre-built jobs — a distinct API from `insert/2`.
